Title: The WISE Catalog of Galactic H II Regions 
Authors: Anderson L.D., Bania T.M., Balser D.S., Cunningham V., Wenger T.V., 
         Johnstone B.M., Armentrout W.P. 
Table: WISE Catalog of Galactic H II Regions
================================================================================
Byte-by-byte Description of file: datafile2.txt
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Bytes Format Units  Label    Explanations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1- 15 A15    ---    WISE     WISE identifier (1)
      17 A1     ---    Class    Classification (2)
  19- 25 F7.3   deg    WGLON    WISE Galactic longitude
  27- 33 F7.3   deg    WGLAT    WISE Galactic latitude 
  35- 38 I4     arcsec R        Radius
  40- 75 A36    ---    HIIName  HII region identifier
  77-112 A36    ---    Mem      Group membership identifier
 114-120 F7.3   deg    HGLON    ? HII region Galactic longitude
 122-128 F7.3   deg    HGLAT    ? HII region Galactic latitude 
 130-153 A24    km/s   VLSR     ? Ionized Gas Local Standard of 
                                  Rest Velocity (3)
 155-170 A16    km/s   eVLSR    ? The 1{sigma} error in VLSR (3)
 172-191 A20    km/s   DelV     ? Ionized Gas FWHM line width (3)
 193-208 A16    km/s   eDelV    ? The 1{sigma} error in DelV (3)
 210-211 A2     ---    Ref      Reference code (4)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note (1): If there are multiple WISE sources within the beam of an observed 
          position, all such WISE sources share the same line parameters.
Note (2): 
    K = Known;
    G = Group;
    C = Candidate;
    Q = radio Quiet;
    ? = no radio data
Note (3): Semicolons indicate multiple values.
Note (4):
    1 = Arvidsson et al. (2009);
    2 = Anderson et al. (2011); 
    3 = Araya et al. (2002);
    4 = Bania et al. (2012); 
    5 = Caswell & Haynes (1987); 
    6 = Downes et al. (1980);
    7 = Fich et al. (1990); 
    8 = Lockman (1989); 
    9 = Lockman et al. (1996); 
   10 = Sewilo et al. (2004b); 
   11 = Watson et al. (2003); 
   12 = Wilson et al. (1970); 
   13 = Wink et al. (1982).
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
